core_concept: Plants: Parts and What They Need
facts: A plant's roots take up water from the soil and anchor the plant so it does not fall over; without roots, a plant cannot stand up or drink.
A leaf collects sunlight to help the plant grow; a plant with no leaves cannot collect sunlight and will not grow.
Plants need water, sunlight, and soil to live and grow; if you put a plant in a dark cupboard with water and soil, it will still not grow well because it needs sunlight.
misconceptions: A plant can grow just fine with only water; sunlight is not necessary.
